state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington is a state in the Pacific Northwest region of the United States.

entity: State University of New York at Oswego
description: Public college in Oswego, New York
description_wiki: State University of New York at Oswego (SUNY Oswego or Oswego State) is a public university in Oswego, New York, United States.

surface_forms: the war was over in the Pacific
description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: federal reserve force of the United States Army
description_wiki: The United States Army Reserve (USAR) is a reserve force of the United States Army.

description: maritime patrol flying boat family
description_wiki: The Consolidated Model 28, more commonly known as the PBY Catalina (U.S. Navy designation), is an American flying boat and amphibious aircraft designed by Consolidated Aircraft in the 1930s and 1940s.

description: city and county seat of Erie County, New York, United States
description_wiki: Buffalo is a city in the U.S. state of New York.

description: American nonprofit organization
air service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Air Force (USAF) is the air service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Mississippi (  MISS-iss-IP-ee) is a state in the Southeastern and Deep South regions of the United States.

description: city in Bexar, Comal, and Medina counties in Texas, United States, that is the seat of Bexar County
description_wiki: San Antonio ( SAN an-TOH-nee-oh; Spanish for "Saint Anthony") is a city in the U.S. state of Texas.

description: city in and county seat of Onondaga County, New York, United States
description_wiki: Syracuse ( SIRR-ə-kewz, SERR-, -⁠kewss) is a city in New York, United States, and the county seat of Onondaga County.

description: human settlement in Bexar County, Texas, United States of America
description_wiki: Lackland Air Force Base  (IATA: SKF, ICAO: KSKF, FAA LID: SKF) is a United States Air Force (USAF) base located in Bexar County, Texas, United States.

description: bomber aircraft family
description_wiki: The Boeing B-52 Stratofortress is an American nuclear-capable subsonic jet-powered strategic bomber.

description: attack aircraft family
description_wiki: The General Dynamics F-111 Aardvark is a retired, supersonic, medium-range fighter-bomber.

description: combat involving electronics and directed energy
description_wiki: Electromagnetic warfare or electronic warfare (EW) is warfare involving the use of the electromagnetic spectrum (EM spectrum) or directed energy to control the spectrum, attack an enemy, or impede enemy operations.

description: United States Air Force base near Homestead, Florida
description_wiki: Homestead Air Reserve Base (IATA: HST, ICAO: KHST, FAA LID: HST) (Homestead ARB), previously known as Homestead Air Force Base (Homestead AFB) is located in Miami–Dade County, Florida, to the northeast of the city of Homestead.

description: US Air Force base at Sumter, South Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Shaw Air Force Base (Shaw AFB) (IATA: SSC, ICAO: KSSC, FAA LID: SSC) is a United States Air Force (USAF) base located approximately 8.4 miles (13.5 km) west-northwest of downtown Sumter, South Carolina.

entity: Korat Royal Thai Air Force Base
description: Royal Thai Air Force base in Nakhon Ratchasima Province, Thailand
description_wiki: Korat Royal Thai Air Force Base or Nakhon Ratchasima Royal Thai Air Force Base is a base of the Royal Thai Air Force (RTAF) in northeast Thailand, approximately 200 km (125 mi) northeast of Bangkok and about 4 km (2.5 mi) south of the centre of the city of Nakhon Ratchasima in the Nakhon Ratchasima Province (also known as "Khorat" or "Korat"), the largest province in Thailand.

description: International airport in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam
description_wiki: Tan Son Nhat International Airport (IATA: SGN, ICAO: VVTS) is an international airport serving Ho Chi Minh City, the most populous city in Vietnam.

description: tanker aircraft family by Boeing
description_wiki: The Boeing KC-135 Stratotanker is an American military aerial refueling tanker aircraft that was developed from the Boeing 367-80 prototype, alongside the Boeing 707 airliner.

description: country in Southeast Asia
description_wiki: Laos, officially the Lao People's Democratic Republic (LPDR), is a country in Mainland Southeast Asia, and the only landlocked country in Southeast Asia.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: 1972 USAF strategic bombing campaign against North Vietnam
description_wiki: Operation Linebacker II, sometimes referred to as the Christmas bombings and, in Vietnam, the Twelve days and nights, or Điện Biên Phủ of the Sky, was a strategic bombing campaign conducted by the United States against military and industrial areas of North Vietnam from 18 December to 29 December 1972, during the Vietnam War.

description: unincorporated territory of the United States of America
description_wiki: Guam (  GWAHM; Chamorro: Guåhan [ˈɡʷɑhɑn]) is an island that is an organized, unincorporated territory of the United States in the Micronesia subregion of the western Pacific Ocean.
